title: By Grand Central Station I Sat Down and Wept
description: A lyrical novel of adulterous passion, longing, and emotional excess.
descriptionParagraphs: A lyrical novel of adulterous passion, longing, and emotional excess.

By Grand Central Station I Sat Down and Wept is usually read through its treatment of desire, adultery, and lyrical prose. As a novel, it turns those concerns into conflicts of character, voice, setting, and social pressure rather than leaving them as abstract ideas.

Part of the work's durability lies in the way its form intensifies its themes. Readers return to it not only for subject matter but for the distinctive voice, structure, and atmosphere through which it makes desire, adultery, and lyrical prose feel immediate.

overview.englishDescription: A lyrical novel of adulterous passion, longing, and emotional excess.
banningParagraphs: By Grand Central Station I Sat Down and Wept entered censorship debates as a novel associated with desire, adultery, and lyrical prose. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity and morality.

The earliest event currently captured here is 1940s in Canada, where Canadian authorities banned circulation. The novel's intensity and adultery scandal drew official suppression. Its current literary status makes the earlier ban look particularly revealing.
This entry is still incomplete: more jurisdictions, court orders, and translated justifications should be added over time.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
